user_message: I have this escalation email from Contoso Ltd that needs create the summary. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 14:20 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Contoso Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 12:21 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 16:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-G trap (Confuses support-ticket scope with CSA scope); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-G failure mode (Confuses support-ticket scope with CSA scope)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-G -- Confuses support-ticket scope with CSA scope
